headline: Claude rebounds sharply while Perplexity crashes; tech targets wildly miss
summary: A dramatic week of divergent model performance saw Claude stage a stunning recovery on March 31st with near-perfect predictions (SPY, MRVL, NVDA, QQQ all scoring 0.9+), while Perplexity imploded on March 30th with catastrophic price target errors (SPY at 6405, QQQ at 23350, DIA at 50300). Grok remained steady but struggled with NVDA, while GPT-4o showed inconsistent conviction. By week's end, market confusion reigned as several models reversed directional calls on SPY and QQQ, highlighting the challenge of short-term equity prediction.

best_call.summary: Claude predicted NVDA at $175 with 60% confidence on March 31st; it closed at $174.40 for a 99.66% target accuracy.

worst_call.summary: Perplexity predicted DIA would rise to 50300 on March 30th when it actually closed at 452.06, a truly catastrophic miss.
